_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass (SWORD VARIANT) — take the L7 'Footman Sergeant' composited keeper and produce the L8 'Footman' variant (Tier 3 mid).
history: v1 (2026-05-07): generated by build_sword_prompts.py from `infantry_edit_L7_to_L8.json` (mace variant). Bulk language substitutions: mace→sword, wooden haft→leather grip, iron spikes→cross-guard, mace head→sword blade, etc. Weapon unchanged at this level — only PRESERVE / POSE language updated to reference the sword.
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chained (Rule 2): two inputs = L7 composite + L1_Sword_Base scale anchor.

prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The first input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the L7 'Footman Sergeant' kit accumulated on top of the L1 base. You MUST preserve every prior feature shown in the first input image: (a) the OPEN-FACE STEEL SALLET HELM with forward-projecting brim worn over the mail coif (the new L8 step ADDS a half-visor BELOW the brim — the helm dome and brim themselves remain unchanged); (b) the FULL STEEL CUIRASS with 3 articulated horizontal lames across the abdomen and a thicker medial ridge (the new L8 step ADDS engraved scrollwork on the medial ridge); (c) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle visible between the tassets at the front; (d) the STEEL TASSETS at both hips (the new L8 step EXTENDS them longer to mid-thigh and adds 3 vertical articulated lames per tasset); (e) the LAYERED 2-DISC STEEL PAULDRONS on both shoulders (the new L8 step ADDS gold rivet heads at the seam between the two layered discs AND ADDS a third lower lame extending down the upper arm to cover the bicep); (f) the STEEL VAMBRACES on both forearms extending continuously into STEEL GAUNTLETS at the wrist (knuckle-plates and articulated finger-plates, no skin visible on the hands); (g) the SOLID FLANGED STEEL SWORD BLADE with 6 vertical flanges (NOT wooden, NOT spiked — the L7 swap from arming sword to flanged steel must remain); (h) the SWORD LEATHER GRIP with leather grip wrap on upper third and two L6 steel grip-rings at the top and bottom of the leather grip; (i) the LARGER STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with embossed sun-burst boss (raised central dome with 8 short triangular rays radiating outward), the four iron cross strap-bands radiating from the boss, the continuous iron rim band with ~16 dark-iron studs, the existing rim rivets; (j) the STEEL SABATONS over warm-brown leather boots; (k) the LIVERY SKIRT BAND visible through the front gap between the two tassets (the new L8 step NARROWS this band as the tassets extend longer, but the red+mustard split is still visible as a narrow ~2-inch band); (l) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH on anatomical-RIGHT hip and the BROWN LEATHER KNIFE SHEATH with cream bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip (visible at the upper edge of each tasset, riding above the new extended tasset top edge); (m) the MAIL COIF back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. DO NOT revert any of these features. The second input image is provided ONLY as a scale and framing reference — match its character-to-canvas proportions exactly; do NOT apply any of its content. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the first input image, marking the mid Tier 3 step from Footman Sergeant to Footman. The unit takes its first gold accent, gains a half-visor that begins concealing the face, and broadens the shoulder + tasset envelope (silhouette mightiness rule active: shoulder line widens with the new third lame; tassets flare longer and gain articulated lames). (1) HALF-VISOR ON THE HELM — add a steel half-visor extending DOWNWARD from the brim of the existing sallet helm to cover the UPPER HALF OF THE FACE (forehead, eyes, eyebrows, upper bridge of nose). The visor is cool silver-gray polished steel matching the helm body, with a single horizontal eye slit running across the visor at eye level (the slit is approximately 1 inch tall, the warm steel-blue eyes are STILL VISIBLE through this slit as two bright eye points within the slit's shadow). The half-visor's lower edge sits at approximately the bridge of the nose, leaving the LOWER HALF of the face visible: the prominent slightly-hooked nose tip, the dark mustache, the full short-trimmed dark-brown beard, and the chin remain fully visible below the visor's bottom edge. The visor has a soft three-dimensional contour following the curve of the upper face, with two small dark-iron rivets at the visor's hinge points where it meets the brim above each eye. The brow ridge of the visor is slightly raised. (2) GOLD RIVET HEADS AT THE PAULDRON SEAM (FIRST GOLD ACCENT ON THE UNIT) — replace the dark-iron rivets at the seam between the two layered discs of each pauldron with BUTTERY WARM GOLD rivet heads. Each pauldron now shows three or four GOLD round rivet heads at the seam where the smaller upper disc joins the larger base disc, distinctly visible against the cool silver-gray steel of the pauldrons. The gold is a saturated warm yellow gold (NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard, NOT brass — buttery polished gold metal) with a soft top-down highlight and a warm shadow on the underside of each rivet head. This is the FIRST gold accent on the unit; the rivets are the only gold added at L8 (other gold accents arrive at L9 / L9.5 / L10). (3) 3-LAME PAULDRONS — ADD A THIRD LOWER LAME extending downward from the existing 2-disc pauldron base disc. The new third lame is a curved steel plate covering the upper arm from the lower edge of the base disc down to roughly mid-bicep, ~3 inches tall, cool silver-gray steel matching the pauldron, with two visible dark-iron rivets at its upper seam where it joins the base disc. Each pauldron now reads as 3 stacked plates: small upper disc (smallest), base disc (medium), bicep lame (longer downward). The shoulder envelope reads visibly BROADER and LONGER than at L7 — the silhouette mightiness rule applies: the shoulder line must clearly extend further outward and downward than at L7. The new third lame covers the previous L4 scale-mail short sleeves on the upper bicep (the scale-mail short sleeves are still visible briefly at the lower edge of the lame as a narrow ~1-inch band of steel scales just above the upper edge of the steel vambraces). (4) LONGER 3-LAME TASSETS EXTENDING TO MID-THIGH WITH OUTWARD FLARE — extend each existing tasset DOWNWARD to cover mid-thigh (each tasset now ~9 inches tall, up from ~6 inches at L6/L7). Each tasset is now articulated as THREE VERTICAL LAMES (steel bands stacked vertically) with thin shadow seams between them. The lower edge of each tasset has a SLIGHT OUTWARD FLARE so the bottom corners are wider than the top corners — the tasset silhouette flares outward at the bottom. The cool silver-gray steel matches the cuirass. The two tassets are still separated at the front by a narrow vertical gap (now ~2 inches at L8, down from ~3 inches at L6); the LIVERY skirt band remains visible through this narrowed front gap as a ~2-inch tall red+mustard strip. The dark blue trousers are still visible at the calf below the lower edge of the longer tassets. (5) ENGRAVED CUIRASS MEDIAL RIDGE — add fine-line scrollwork engraving running vertically along the cuirass medial ridge from the collar (where it joins the bevor) down to the belt line. The engraving is a delicate hand-painted line of stylized leaf and curl motifs, rendered in cool silver-gray steel with subtly darker incised lines (NO gold, NO color — pure tonal engraving on steel). The engraving runs the length of the medial ridge, including across the three abdominal lames. Subtle but adds richness to the cuirass face. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE FIRST INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the first input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline (still visible below the new half-visor); his stern flat closed-mouth expression (mouth visible below the visor); his thick dark-brown eyebrows (NOW HIDDEN BY THE HALF-VISOR — eyebrows are concealed at L8); his large warm steel-blue eyes (NOW VISIBLE ONLY through the horizontal eye slit as two bright eye points in the visor's shadow); his prominent slightly-hooked nose with the rosy painted tip (the upper bridge of the nose is concealed by the visor's lower edge; the nose tip remains visible); his warm peachy-tan skin tone with rosy blush (visible on the lower face / cheeks below the visor); his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering chin and lower cheeks (visible below the visor) — trimmed short, NOT bushy, NOT wild; his dark mustache (visible below the visor); his dark-brown short hair fringe (NOW HIDDEN BY THE HELM AND VISOR — no fringe visible at L8); the mail coif's back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm; the bevor / upper-chest steel; the LIVERY skirt band visible through the narrowed front gap between the two tassets; the wide leather belt with iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip; the brown leather knife sheath with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip; the steel vambraces extending into steel gauntlets with knuckle-plates and articulated finger-plates; the leather grip wrap on the sword; the two steel grip-rings on the sword leather grip; the sword leather grip (the sword HEAD is the L7 flanged steel head — wooden head + spikes are GONE); the larger steel-faced round shield with embossed sun-burst boss, four iron cross strap-bands, continuous iron rim band, ~16 rim studs, central steel boss; the steel sabatons over warm-brown leather boots; the dark blue / muted steel-blue trousers visible at the calf. ===== POSE (must match the first input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE SWORD ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, gauntleted hand grips the sword's leather grip, longsword blade extending downward and toward viewer-LEFT with the tip near hip height. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height. Calm guard stance. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin (lower face only), saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Match the framing of the first input image as closely as possible (the first input has slight tier-break growth from L1_Base — vfill ~70% — that growth is acceptable; the new L8 features should not push framing further). DO NOT zoom in further, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT close the visor fully — the L8 visor is a HALF-VISOR covering only the upper half of the face; the lower face (nose tip, mustache, beard, chin) MUST remain fully visible (closed visor arrives at L9); DO NOT cover the eyes opaque — eyes must still be visible as bright steel-blue points through the horizontal eye slit; DO NOT make the eye slit a vertical slit or a cross slit — single horizontal slit; DO NOT extend the visor below the bridge of the nose; DO NOT remove the helm dome / brim — the helm body is preserved; DO NOT add horns, crest, plumes, or any vertical helm extension (horns arrive at L9, central crest at L10); DO NOT extend the gold to other parts of the unit — gold at L8 is ONLY the rivet heads at the pauldron seam; DO NOT add gold trim, gold band, gold buckle, gold knuckle-plates, gold sword, gold shield rim, or any other gold at L8 (gold expands at L9 / L9.5 / L10); DO NOT change the rivet color from gold to brass or yellow paint — the rivets are buttery POLISHED GOLD METAL; DO NOT make the third pauldron lame a fourth disc — exactly 3 stacked plates per pauldron at L8 (4-lame with sculpted lion's-head motif arrives at L10); DO NOT cover the entire upper arm with the lame — the lame ends at mid-bicep with the scale-mail short sleeve briefly visible below it (the L4 scale sleeves are still there beneath the new lame, visible as a ~1-inch band before the vambraces); DO NOT extend the tassets beyond mid-thigh; DO NOT make the tassets one continuous skirt — they remain TWO SEPARATE plates with a vertical gap at the front; DO NOT shrink the livery skirt band to nothing — it must remain visible as a ~2-inch red+mustard strip through the narrowed front gap; DO NOT make the cuirass engraving gold or colored — the engraving is pure tonal scrollwork on steel; DO NOT make the engraving a heraldic emblem, cross, or motif — it is a fine-line scrollwork (leaves and curls); DO NOT add a closed visor, full-face visor, or face cover other than the half-visor; DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT change the boss color to gold — the sun-burst boss remains cool silver-gray steel at L8 (gold ring around the boss arrives at L9); DO NOT add a cross emblem to the shield — that arrives at L9.5; DO NOT change the sword blade shape — the L7 flanged steel head with 6 vertical flanges is preserved; DO NOT make the sword blade gold or partially gold — gold sword arrives at L10; DO NOT replace the sword leather grip with metal — the sword grip remains wood; DO NOT change any facial feature visible below the visor; DO NOT grow the beard bushy or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color (boots remain warm-brown leather where the sabatons do not cover); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the sword; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
